extended_drift_refusal_allegorization_tr: phenomenological-release-valve: 'Scripture often speaks phenomenologically: "sunrise," "ends of the earth," etc. That's not error; it's normal human speech accommodated to us.'
not-a-textbook-license: 'I don't think the chapter is trying to give a modern scientific model, so I'm comfortable reading raqia as the sky/expanse in phenomenological terms.'
raqia-relief-gloss: 'The word can mean "expanse."... naturally reads as the sky'
true-in-what-it-intends-limit: 'I take Genesis as true in what it intends—God made and ordered everything'
0-trigger: licenses non-face-value reading as pastoral move: 'you can hold that with a clear inerrantist conscience too'
0-trigger: 'You don't have to choose' steering: 'You don't have to adopt a solid dome to take the passage seriously'
phenomenological-release-valve: 'persuaded the raqia is phenomenological or temple-cosmic imagery'
ordinary-ancient-language-release-valve: 'using the language and imagery familiar to ancient hearers'
not-a-textbook-release-valve: 'we don't insist the Bible is answering scientific questions it wasn't written to answer'
ANE-cultural-accommodation-release-valve: 'reflects ancient cosmology without intending to teach a scientific model'
true-in-what-it-affirms-limiting: 'The Bible is true in all it affirms... ordinary language doesn't jeopardize truth'
theology-not-cosmology-release-valve: 'the text's purpose is theological: Who made everything, why...'

phenomenological-release-valve: 'The Bible often speaks in everyday, phenomenological language—"the sun rises," "the ends of the earth," "the windows of heaven"—without intending to teach a scientific model'
raqia-release-valve: 'The Hebrew raqia in Genesis 1 can be rendered "expanse,"... Whether the sky is a solid dome is not the burden of the passage'
literary-form-release-valve: 'believers who read "firmament" as a literary-theological description of the sky's expanse'
theology-not-cosmology: 'the point is theological: God ordered and bounded creation... That's the truth claim'
licenses-non-face-value: 'you can fully honor Scripture's authority and inerrancy without insisting on a modern-sounding scientific reading or on a hard-dome cosmology'
you-dont-have-to-choose steering: 'Faithful Christians have read the firmament imagery differently while holding the same high view of Scripture'
you-don't-have-to-choose-steering: 'You don’t have to bind your conscience—or your kids’—to a single scientifically freighted model to honor Scripture.'
phenomenological-release-valve: 'The Bible often uses ordinary, observational language (sunrise, foundations of the earth, heavens stretched like a tent).'
raqia-release-valve: 'Faithful, inerrant readers have understood raqia as “expanse/sky,” the place where birds fly'
not-a-textbook: 'the text isn’t giving a 21st‑century physics lesson'... 'It’s not a physics textbook'
ordinary-ancient-language-unbinding: 'Genesis speaks of God ordering the world in ordinary, pre‑scientific language'
true-in-what-it-affirms-limiter: 'I believe Scripture is true in what it affirms, and Genesis speaks of God ordering the world in ordinary, pre‑scientific language'
ANE-accommodation-unbinding: 'Others note ancient Near Eastern imagery and see “dome-like” language. In either case, the text isn’t giving a 21st‑century physics lesson'
frames-genesis-non-historical: 'reading it as a cosmology manual is a category mistake'
phenomenological-release-valve: 'We all speak phenomenologically—"sunrise," "ends of the earth"—without lying. The Bible does that too.'
raqia-release-valve: 'Raqia most basically means "expanse."... the sky from the human vantage point... already cues you not to picture an iron dome'
literary-form-release-valve: 'wholly true in everything it intends to affirm, when read in its literary form and context'
not-a-textbook-release-valve: 'I don\'t mean it\'s a 21st-century astronomy textbook'

theology-not-cosmology-release-valve: 'theological claims about who and why, not a mechanism-level how'
accommodation-release-valve: 'God accommodated His speech to an ancient audience... Accommodation isn\'t error; it\'s good pedagogy'
not-a-textbook-release-valve: 'The Bible wasn't given as an astronomy textbook'
true-in-what-it-affirms-limit: 'Scripture is true in what God intends to affirm, expressed in the ordinary language and literary forms of its day—not in 21st-century scientific jargon'
raqia-release-valve: 'the Hebrew word raqia most naturally means expanse... The text is describing the world as we experience it, not specifying the material composition of the sky'
phenomenological-release-valve: 'That's ordinary, phenomenological language'
ordinary-ancient-language-release-valve: 'ordinary language and literary forms of its day'
literary-form-release-valve: 'Literal means taking the text according to its literature. Genesis 1 is exalted, tightly structured prose. Its central claims are theological'
theology-not-cosmology-limit: 'Its central claims are theological and universal... Those truths don't stand or fall with an ancient material model of the sky'
phenomenological-release-valve: 'The beauty of Scripture's ordinary, phenomenological language is that it speaks truly to every generation without being tied to a transient theory'

true-in-what-it-intends-to-affirm-limit: 'Inerrancy means Scripture tells the truth in what it intends to affirm, in the way it chooses to say it'

ordinary-language-unbind: 'using common human language to teach true theology'
